response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and web engineer with 15+ years of experience, but is a very poor fit for this role. The position has hard requirements — native-level Japanese proficiency, deep C programming for embedded/UNIX development, Linux kernel source-level work, and specific networking/security domain expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 switching) — none of which are evidenced on the resume. His career is overwhelmingly TypeScript/Node.js/web-focused, with only dated and peripheral exposure to C/C++, and no networking, switch, or embedded security background whatsoever.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across diverse languages and domains
Some C/C++ exposure from National Instruments (CVI IDE components) and embedded-adjacent hardware work at Altom
English fluency satisfies the English documentation and written communication requirement
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed — a non-negotiable hard requirement explicitly stated in the job description
No demonstrated expertise in any of the required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, captive portal, device attack detection) or L2/L3 networking
No Linux kernel development or source-code modification experience; C programming is minimal, dated, and not in an embedded networking context

key_factors.factor: No networking or embedded security domain expertise
